claim: LangGraph is the production standard for complex, stateful, multi-agent workflows requiring deterministic execution, audit trails, and human-in-the-loop approval gates.
CrewAI is the fastest-growing and most-starred Python agent framework, prized for its intuitive role-based abstraction.
CrewAI is fully standalone from LangChain and has a 5.76x speed advantage over LangGraph in certain QA tasks.
CrewAI claims 100,000+ certified developers and use by 63% of the Fortune 500.
OpenAI Agents SDK is the cleanest implementation of the handoff-based orchestration model: agents pass control explicitly via specialized tool calls.
Claude Agent SDK offers the deepest MCP integration of any framework — a single line of config connects to 200+ MCP servers.
Anthropic announced a billing change splitting programmatic usage (Agent SDK, claude -p, GitHub Actions) into a separate credit pool, paused as of June 15 pending redesign.
Microsoft Agent Framework (MAF) 1.0 GA April 3, 2026, is the production successor to both AutoGen and Semantic Kernel.
Google ADK ships in Python, TypeScript, Go, Java, and Kotlin — more languages than any competing framework.
Mastra is the leading TypeScript-native agent framework — 60–70% of YC X25 agent startups build in TypeScript.
Agno claims ~10,000x faster agent instantiation than LangGraph and ~50x less memory.
DSPy can improve structured task accuracy by 10–40% over manual prompting via signatures, modules, and teleprompters.
Smolagents core agent logic fits in ~1,000 lines of code — agents write Python code as their action primitive, using 30% fewer steps than traditional tool-calling agents.
MCP (Model Context Protocol) for agent-to-tool and A2A (Agent-to-Agent) for inter-agent coordination have become de facto standards, supported by all major frameworks.
Framework choice can affect agent accuracy by up to 30 percentage points on identical models (HAL benchmark).
60%+ of LangGraph production incidents trace to state management failures.
LangGraph's checkpointing with time-travel (replay any prior state) is essential for debugging and compliance.
CrewAI rates medium on production readiness — debugging multi-agent handoffs remains challenging.
MAF 1.0 supports six model providers (Azure OpenAI, OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Amazon Bedrock, Google Gemini, Ollama) with one-line switching.
OpenAI Agents SDK tracing is built-in (Agents Tracing UI) with first-class sandbox execution.
Google ADK four-layer context architecture (Static, Turn, User, Cache) is more sophisticated than any competitor's approach to token management.
Vertex AI Agent Engine provides managed scaling with sub-100ms cold starts.
Mastra workflow engine (.then(), .branch(), .parallel()) is production-grade, though dual-license model creates uncertainty.
Enterprise agent framework adoption grew 200% between 2025 and 2026 (Forrester).
JPMorgan Chase deployed LangGraph-based agents for trade settlement, reducing manual intervention by 90%.
Klarna operates a LangGraph-based customer service agent serving 85M users.
CrewAI's Flows system (event-driven, production-grade workflows) and Crews (autonomous agent teams) address both prototyping speed and production needs.
LangGraph's TypedDict-based state with reducers enables parallel agent execution without race conditions.
LangSmith provides tracing, evaluation, and monitoring — a complete observability stack that no other framework matches.
Smolagents code agents use 30% fewer steps than JSON tool-calling agents on standardized benchmarks.
DSPy optimization pipeline typically adds 1–3 compilation cycles (minutes to hours) but reduces per-query cost through better prompt efficiency.
CrewAI wins decisively on fastest-to-prototype — zero to working multi-agent system in 2–4 hours.
LangGraph and DSPy tie for steepest learning curve — LangGraph requires graph theory concepts before agent logic.
LangGraph Studio provides visual graph debugging, time-travel debugging, and state inspection at every node.
Claude Agent SDK billing changes potentially increase costs 5–25x for heavy users depending on usage patterns.
LLM costs dominate total cost of ownership — framework licensing is negligible compared to model inference costs.
Framework overhead (extra tokens for system prompts, tool schemas, state serialization) adds 5–20% to LLM costs.
MCP and A2A protocols transform agent tooling from proprietary interfaces to a standardized ecosystem.
A2A merged with ACP under the Linux Foundation in 2026, creating a single cross-framework communication standard.
Framework differentiation is shifting from tool support to orchestration quality — tools built as MCP servers are portable across frameworks.
The 'no framework' argument has merit for simple use cases — single LLM call with one or two tool calls does not need a framework.
LangGraph leads on enterprise-grade state management with 300+ enterprise customers.
CrewAI supports both MCP and A2A protocols natively.
OpenAI Agents SDK supports 100+ LLMs and is provider-agnostic but optimized for OpenAI's models.
Google ADK powers Google's own agents in Agentspace and the Customer Engagement Suite.
Mastra integrates with Replit Agent (thousands of Mastra agents created daily), SoftBank, PayPal, and Marsh McLennan.
Agno's AgentOS runtime wraps agents as FastAPI-based REST APIs with session management, tracing, and RBAC built-in.
DSPy is used in production at Databricks, Shopify, and Dropbox with 6.4M+ monthly downloads.
LlamaIndex has evolved from a pure RAG framework into a broader agentic platform with Workflows (event-driven orchestration) and 160+ data connectors.
Haystack is used in production by Airbus, NVIDIA, Comcast, and The Economist — its modular architecture allows independent component testing.
Strands Agents reached v1.0 May 2026 with multi-agent patterns (Swarm, Graph, Workflow, A2A) and 14M+ downloads in under a year.
AG2 maintains backward compatibility with AutoGen v0.2 while adding event-driven architecture and async message passing.
Microsoft placed original AutoGen (57,722 stars) in maintenance mode, directing new users to MAF.
La ngGraph's explicit graph model gives strongest guarantees for deterministic execution — every transition checkpointed, every state change auditable.
Vertex AI Memory Bank pricing is transparent and predictable at $0.25/1,000 memories/month.
OpenAI Agents SDK is the cleanest API design — fewest primitives (Agent, Runner, Guardrails, Handoffs).
Smolagents is the most Pythonic framework — core in ~1,000 lines, hackable and transparent.
Mastra has the best TypeScript API — Zod-based tool schemas, type-safe workflows, native Node.js patterns.
CrewAI is the worst debugging experience — role-based abstraction becomes opaque in multi-agent pipelines.
Agno benchmark claims should be interpreted cautiously — LLM latency dominates in practice, not framework overhead.
IDC projects the agent framework market at 34% CAGR through 2027.
CrewAI Control Plane (enterprise tier) adds tracing, RBAC, audit trails, and human-in-the-loop approval gates.
Switching from CrewAI to LangGraph after 6 months means rewriting state management, tool integrations, and evaluation pipelines.
OpenAI Agents SDK April 15, 2026 update added native sandbox execution, resumable state, and configurable memory.
Google ADK evaluation framework is more mature than most competitors, with built-in trajectory evaluation tools.
CrewAI's independence from LangChain gives it a substantial speed advantage — framework is fully standalone.
Strands Agents is the newest entrant, offering a model-driven, minimal-agent-loop approach with native integration to Amazon Bedrock, Lambda, and Step Functions.
Haystack's modular pipeline design allows each component to be tested, replaced, and improved independently — significant for regulated industries.
The framework market is stratifying, not converging — coordination models (graph, role-based, conversational, handoff) are diverging into distinct architectural philosophies.
LangGraph's v1.2 release (May 2026) added content-block-aware streaming and improved interrupt semantics.
